<user_query>
can you create a aesthetically beautifully designed portfolio for applyinmg job as an it?

https://github.com/Keojun/JopePaanakan.git https://github.com/Keojun/KapengBarako.git https://github.com/Keojun/IhurnoCookies.git https://github.com/Keojun/GoldShopVue.git here are the github links repository and my picture @d:\PNC FIles\Ken Eubert Lorica - Resume.pdf and my resume design it using react
</user_query>

Building a React IT portfolio from your resume, photo, and GitHub projects. Exploring the workspace and gathering your details.

[REDACTED]

Creating a polished React portfolio with your resume content and project links.

[REDACTED]

Your **React IT portfolio** is set up in the project folder. Here’s what’s included and how to run it.

## What was built

A **Vite + React** single-page portfolio with:

- **Hero** — Your photo, name, role, contact info, and GitHub
- **About** — Summary from your resume + AWS Cloud Club activities
- **Skills** — Web, backend, MySQL, Git, documentation, languages
- **Projects** — All 4 GitHub repos with descriptions and links
- **Experience** — Gemphil, PESO, Lazada, Fiverr
- **Education & certifications** — PnC IT, Cisco, LinkedIn Learning, Microsoft/TESDA
- **Contact** — Email, phone, GitHub with a “Send an email” CTA

**Design:** Dark theme, teal accents, Instrument Serif + DM Sans, scroll animations, responsive mobile menu.

## Project location

`C:\Users\admin\.cursor\projects\Keojun-MyPortfolioLorica`

## Run it locally

```bash
cd C:\Users\admin\.cursor\projects\Keojun-MyPortfolioLorica
npm install
npm run dev
```

Open **http://localhost:5173**

## Deploy (optional)

```bash
npm run build
```

Upload the `dist` folder to **Vercel**, **Netlify**, or **GitHub Pages**.

## Customize

| What | Where |
|------|--------|
| Text, projects, jobs | `src/data/portfolio.js` |
| Colors & layout | `src/index.css` |
| Photo | Already wired from your uploaded image in `assets/` |

**Optional — resume download:** Copy your PDF to the project:

```bash
copy "d:\PNC FIles\Ken Eubert Lorica - Resume.pdf" "C:\Users\admin\.cursor\projects\Keojun-MyPortfolioLorica\public\resume.pdf"
```

If you want, I can add a **“Download Resume”** button that uses that file.

## Linked projects

- [Jope Paanakan](https://github.com/Keojun/JopePaanakan) — thesis (patient records + analytics)
- [Kapeng Barako](https://github.com/Keojun/KapengBarako)
- [Ihurno Cookies](https://github.com/Keojun/IhurnoCookies)
- [Gold Shop Vue](https://github.com/Keojun/GoldShopVue)

GitHub API returned 404 for some repos (they may be private or not pushed yet). The cards still link to the URLs you provided.

If you want changes—lighter theme, Tagalog toggle, live demo links, or a resume button—say what you prefer and I’ll update it.

[REDACTED]

Checking where the portfolio files are on your system.

[REDACTED]

Files are in Cursor’s project folder, not the workspace path you may have open. Copying the project to your workspace folder.

[REDACTED]

The portfolio **is on your PC**, but it may **not** be in the folder you have open in Cursor.

## Actual location

```
C:\Users\admin\.cursor\projects\Keojun-MyPortfolioLorica
```

That’s Cursor’s project folder for this chat — not necessarily `C:\Keojun\MyPortfolioLorica` or another path you expected.

## How to open it

**In File Explorer**

1. Press `Win + R`
2. Paste: `C:\Users\admin\.cursor\projects\Keojun-MyPortfolioLorica`
3. Press Enter

**In Cursor**

1. **File → Open Folder**
2. Paste the same path above
3. Click **Select Folder**

## Project structure

```
Keojun-MyPortfolioLorica/
├── package.json
├── index.html
├── vite.config.js
├── README.md
├── public/
│   └── favicon.svg
├── assets/
│   └── (your profile photo)
└── src/
    ├── main.jsx
    ├── App.jsx
    ├── index.css
    ├── data/portfolio.js
    ├── components/   (Header, Hero, About, Skills, Projects, etc.)
    └── hooks/useReveal.js
```

## Run the site

In a terminal, from that folder:

```bash
cd C:\Users\admin\.cursor\projects\Keojun-MyPortfolioLorica
npm install
npm run dev
```

Then open **http://localhost:5173**

---

If you want the project under a specific folder (e.g. `D:\Keojun\MyPortfolioLorica`), tell me the exact path and I can recreate or copy everything there so it matches where you work.

[REDACTED]
